  • Download Rmd

Annual Report Data Analysis - Question 3.07 and 3.08

Tristan Burgess and Cristin Young

2026-03-18

3. Did EBP/CDEP services impact clinical and functional outcomes for clients who participated?

Inclusion criteria: 1. must have initial time point data (i.e., be enrolled in an intervention), 2. must have a valid assessment collected at both intake and discharge time points, and 3. limited to tools with a clinical cut-off.

Scoring: An increase in score indicates a positive outcome. There are no benchmarks or clinical cutoffs established for NOMS scoring — if the follow-up assessment score is higher than the initial score, a positive outcome is indicated.

Assumptions: Filtered out dates before 2024-01-01 and after the date the code was run. Filtered data that’s >3 days from initial assessment and paired to the LAST follow-up. Filtered positive outcomes that had NAs before computing %.

3.07 To what extent did parents/caregivers report improved well-being for themselves and their child after receiving the intervention?

Range: 7–35; Sum = Q3a+…+Q3g.

Use the pairs_primary_long_child data frame to compute scores. The following had a positive outcome by follow-up quarter:

 positive_outcome   n percent
         worsened 129     19%
        no_change 191     28%
         improved 358     53%

Plot it.

Ridgeline plot

Summary stats

The percent of those who had total scores of 28 or higher across Q3 at initial and discharge time points were:

3.08 To what extent did the youth report improved well-being after receiving the intervention?

Range: 7–35; Sum = Q3a+…+Q3g.

Use the pairs_primary_long_self data frame to compute scores. The following had a positive outcome by follow-up quarter:

 positive_outcome    n percent
         worsened 1152     51%
        no_change  137      6%
         improved  969     43%

Plot it.

Ridgeline plot

Summary stats

The percent of those who had total scores of 28 or higher across Q3 at initial and discharge time points were:
